summary: An area qualifies as being part of a high-endemic region according to the WHO when it has over ten new cases annually out of every hundred thousand individuals due primarily to increased frequency leading towards higher risk factors within such populations. The specific threshold mentioned by WHO helps identify these zones accurately based purely upon epidemiological metrics related specifically toward this type of infectious disease spread patterns observed globally across different geographical locations impacting public health significantly through varying degrees depending largely from one place another influenced heavily local environmental
